fields.description:
## Why
R1's asserted-capability probe (plan-review) flags a plan that CLAIMS a capability an existing module lacks. R5 adds the Pass-2 verification counterpart in the SHARED kernel: a dedicated `asserted_capability_confirmed` sub-answer so the verifier can confirm/refute a capability-gap finding on the evidence instead of overloading `no_viable_alternative_explanation`. The kernel vocabulary is SHARED with code-review (`src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py:31 GRADED_BINARY`), so this is the epic's highest-blast-radius change and sits behind the strictest gates (E5 byte-identical replay + E6 self-consistency κ=0.874).

## What
Add `asserted_capability_confirmed` to the shared kernel `GRADED_BINARY` (decide.py:31) as an **na-DEFAULT** graded VALIDITY sub-answer (registered in `verify_models._BINARY_NA_DEFAULT`), with the SAME `yes=finding-holds` polarity as every other graded sub-answer (`validity()`, decide.py:56 — yes=1.0, insufficient=0.5, no=0.0):
- `yes` = the review CONFIRMS the asserted gap (the capability really is absent/unwired) — the finding HOLDS, validity retained;
- `no` = the capability is in fact delivered/exercised, REFUTING the finding — validity drops so a FALSE non-delivery claim is dropped;
- `na` (default) everywhere OUTSIDE the G6/E4/T3 asserted-capability cohort → abstains from the graded mean.
Because `_build_binary` (verify_models.py) is shared, BOTH the plan-review and code-review Binary models inherit it. Landed directly in the packaged kernel (no config flag), on E5 + E6 pass.

## Design reconciliation (resolves plan-review G6 + T8 on this ticket)
- **Validity axis, not impact promotion — deliberate.** `docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md:197` sketches feeding a `no` into `divergent_implementation` to LIFT priority. That is the PLAN-review R1 direction (promote a plan's forward claim that later forces redesign). R5 is the CODE-review verification counterpart: at code-review time the capability's presence is DIRECTLY checkable against the diff, so the honest, non-destabilising move is to CONFIRM/REFUTE the finding on the validity axis (weeding false non-delivery claims), NOT to inflate priority. A genuine confirmed gap is surfaced by the finding's OWN impact axes; R5 only prevents a false capability-claim from riding on unearned validity. This is why a `no` correctly drives toward dropped — the capability was delivered, so the non-delivery finding is a false positive.
- **No cross-field coupling.** R5 introduces NO rule that co-populates `divergent_implementation` (a `PlanSeverityAttrs` severity axis) from this binary. The research-doc coupling is explicitly NOT replicated; the sub-answer's only effect is through `validity()`. Recorded in `docs/review-kernel.md`.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` — one entry in `GRADED_BINARY`.
- `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify_models.py` — `_BINARY_DESC` + `_BINARY_NA_DEFAULT` entry. (NEW module: the Pass-2 model builders were extracted from `verify.py` along the existing call-graph seam to hold the LOCKED 800-LOC hard cap — `verify.py` sat at exactly 800; names re-exported from `verify`, no import site changed.)
- `tests/unit/test_r5_asserted_capability.py` — invariance + registration + polarity tests.
- `docs/review-kernel.md` — na-default sub-answer + validity-axis-vs-research reconciliation + no-bump note.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gate-row evidence committed before landing: E5 byte-identical replay artifact (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e5_nonregression_diff.json` — empty decision/priority/validity diff over the 161-row code-review corpus, `na` outside G6/E4/T3) AND E6 Exp A κ≥0.6 (recorded κ=0.874 PASS).
- [ ] `asserted_capability_confirmed` added to the shared `GRADED_BINARY` as an na-default graded sub-answer with `yes=finding-holds` polarity; both plan-review and code-review Binary models default it to `na`. Pinned by `tests/unit/test_r5_asserted_capability.py` (`test_field_registered_and_na_default`, `test_cohort_answer_contributes_to_validity`).
- [ ] Code-review behaviour pinned UNCHANGED by a regression test (the invariance property in `test_r5_asserted_capability.py` + the E5 replay driver): validity/decision byte-identical whether the field is absent or `na`.
- [ ] IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION deliberately NOT bumped — grounded correction of the stub AC: the field is a validity axis touching neither `impact_code` nor `impact_plan` SHAPE (`code_review/sidecar.py:34` bumps only on impact_code shape change), and the byte-identical property (E5) requires sidecars stay un-segmented. Rationale in `docs/review-kernel.md` + the E5 writeup. No cross-field coupling to `divergent_implementation` (resolves G6/T8).
- [ ] Landed directly in the packaged kernel (no config flag) through Gerrit (LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1); module-size hard-800 respected; `docs/review-kernel.md` updated.
